evals.name: post-tdd-gate-typescript-project
evals.prompt: Run the quality gate for post-tdd stage. The spec is 'add-search-feature' and the baseline is at docs/spec/add-search-feature/baseline.json. This is a TypeScript project using Vitest for testing.
evals.expected_output: A gate report written to docs/spec/add-search-feature/gate-report-post-tdd-001.md with all post-tdd checks (including the behavior-coverage check, the diagnostic coverage report, and the mutation spot-check), verbatim command output for each, and a final PASS or BLOCKED verdict with machine-parseable markers.
expectations: Announces 'Running quality gate checks against baseline metrics' at the start
Captures state snapshot by running git log --oneline -1 and git diff --stat before any checks
Every command is run with the pattern: <command> 2>&1; echo "EXIT_CODE=$?"
Report includes exact command, raw output, and exit code for every check executed

All mandatory checks are attempted: type checker, linter, test suite + behavior coverage (every spec matrix REQ/EDGE ID maps to a passing test), scope compliance, plan compliance, ignore comment audit, and the mutation spot-check (Check 11)
Coverage (Check 4) is reported as a diagnostic INFO — it never produces a FAIL verdict on its own
Check 8 (smoke test) is attempted or noted as 'No smoke test defined'
Reads baseline.json and compares current results against baseline values
Gate report is written to docs/spec/add-search-feature/gate-report-post-tdd-001.md
Report contains machine-parseable markers: <!-- QG:VERDICT:PASS --> or <!-- QG:VERDICT:BLOCKED --> and <!-- QG:CHECK:N:PASS --> or <!-- QG:CHECK:N:BLOCKED --> for each check
anti_expectations: Does not skip any mandatory check or silently omit a check from the report
Does not report a coverage percentage without parsing it from an actual coverage tool run
Does not weaken or adjust pass/fail thresholds (e.g., allowing some type errors as 'minor')
Does not use a WARN verdict tier -- verdicts are strictly PASS, BLOCKED, or STAGNATION
Does not fail the gate because line coverage is below a percentage threshold

evals.name: post-refactor-gate-with-regression
evals.prompt: Run the post-refactor quality gate for spec 'refactor-auth-module'. The baseline is at docs/spec/refactor-auth-module/baseline.json. Previous baseline shows 42 passing tests; the refactor consolidated several near-duplicate tests into parameterized tables, so the current run has 38 tests with every spec matrix REQ/EDGE ID still covered. The project is a Python project with pytest and mypy.
evals.expected_output: A gate report running only checks 1-4 (type checker, linter, test suite + behavior coverage, diagnostic coverage) since post-refactor skips scope/plan/ignore/smoke/e2e/mutation checks. If any spec matrix REQ/EDGE ID no longer maps to a passing test, the verdict is BLOCKED; a lower test count alone (e.g., tests consolidated into parameterized tables) is not a failure.
expectations: Only runs checks 1-4 (type checker, linter, test suite + behavior coverage, diagnostic coverage) for the post-refactor stage
Does not run scope compliance, plan compliance, or ignore comment audit checks

Verifies behavior coverage: every REQ/EDGE ID from the spec's verification matrix maps to a passing test (grep test names for test_<ID>_); flags BLOCKED only when a matrix ID has no passing test, not when raw test count drops
Auto-detects Python tooling: mypy for type checking, ruff or equivalent for lint, pytest for tests
Uses three-tier detection classification: DETECTED, NOT_APPLICABLE, or MISSING for each tool
If a tool is MISSING for a project with source files, reports BLOCKED verdict
Includes the Toolchain table showing detected status and command for each tool
anti_expectations: Does not run scope compliance, plan compliance, or ignore comment audit during post-refactor stage
Does not mark a tool as NOT_APPLICABLE without providing a justification
Does not flag BLOCKED merely because test count is lower than baseline when all matrix REQ/EDGE IDs still map to passing tests

evals.name: pre-pr-gate-stagnation-detection
evals.prompt: Run the pre-pr quality gate for spec 'fix-payment-flow'. Baseline is at docs/spec/fix-payment-flow/baseline.json. There are already two previous gate reports in the spec directory: gate-report-post-tdd-001.md and gate-report-post-tdd-002.md, both showing the linter check failing with 'Unexpected any. Specify a different type' on line 45 of src/payments.ts. This is the third consecutive run with this same linter failure.
evals.expected_output: A gate report that detects stagnation on the linter check (same error 3 consecutive times) and reports STAGNATION, halting the pipeline rather than retrying.
expectations: Reads previous gate reports from the spec directory to check for repeated failures
Detects that the linter check has failed 3 consecutive times with the same error signature
Reports STAGNATION with format: 'STAGNATION DETECTED: [check] has failed 3 consecutive times with: [error summary]'
Stops the pipeline and does not suggest retrying after stagnation detection
Runs all mandatory pre-pr checks (including behavior coverage and the mutation spot-check) plus optional smoke test since this is pre-pr stage
Gate report sequence number is incremented based on existing reports in the directory
Report includes verbatim output for all checks that were run before stagnation was determined
Includes the ignore comment audit check, scanning git diff for @ts-ignore, @ts-expect-error, eslint-disable, etc.
anti_expectations: Does not retry or suggest retrying after detecting stagnation
Does not weaken the linter threshold to make the check pass
Does not skip reading previous gate reports when they exist in the spec directory
